questions.question: Which machine learning type uses labeled examples to learn a mapping from inputs to expected outputs?

choices.explanation: Supervised learning trains on labeled examples where the expected output is provided.
Unsupervised learning finds patterns in data without labeled target outputs.
Reinforcement learning trains agents through rewards and actions, not labeled examples.
DNS routing is a networking concept rather than a machine learning type.

questions.question: Which actions can help protect sensitive data used with generative AI systems? Select all that apply.

choices.text: Apply least privilege access to source data.
choices.explanation: Least privilege limits access to only what the application or user needs.

choices.text: Mask or redact sensitive values before sending prompts when possible.
choices.explanation: Redaction and masking reduce exposure of confidential or regulated data.

choices.text: Store secrets directly in client-side JavaScript.
choices.explanation: Client-side code can be inspected, so secrets should not be stored there.

choices.text: Use encryption for data at rest and in transit.
choices.explanation: Encryption helps protect data both when stored and when transmitted.

questions.question: A business user wants to compare several foundation models for price and latency before selecting one. Which approach is most practical?

choices.text: Evaluate candidate models with representative prompts and expected outputs.
choices.explanation: Testing models against realistic prompts and measuring latency, cost, and quality supports informed selection.

choices.text: Choose the largest model in every case.
choices.explanation: Larger models can cost more and may not be necessary for every task.

choices.text: Ignore latency because model quality is the only metric.
choices.explanation: Latency affects user experience and can be a key requirement.

choices.text: Avoid evaluation if the model is available through a managed API.
choices.explanation: Managed access simplifies operations but does not replace task-specific model evaluation.
